name: He Qia (Yangshi)
page_title: He Qia (Yangshi) 和洽 (陽士) [Wei] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
ranks: Prefect of the Gentleman of the Palace
historical_notes:
He Qia, styled Yangshi, was from Xiping county, Runan commandery.

He was initially nominated Filial and Incorrupt by Xu Shao and invited to appointment by He Jin, but he refused to go. In about 190 when many gentry were traveling to Yuan Shao’s Ji province, He Qia, suspecting that Ji would soon succumb to war, instead traveled to Jing.

He Qia joined Cao Cao’s staff in 208. There he argued against the rigidity of the sumptuary codes applied by Cui Yan and Mao Jie. Later in 216, serving as Palace Attendant, he offered support to Mao Jie, rescuing him from jail, after Cui Yan had been put to death.

When Cao Cao died in AD, He Qia served as Gentlemen of the Palace overseeing household security for the kingdom of Wei. He helped to arrange a smooth succession for Cao Pi and was among the sponsors of a stele commemorating Cao Pi taking the imperial title. As a result he was enfeoffed and named Minister of the Household in the new empire. He would later receive additional honors from Cao Rui.

1: Source: [CITE=RAFE:TOME]

full_text:
He Qia (Yangshi) 和洽 (陽士)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
He Qia (和洽), styledYangshi (陽士), was born in Xiping in Runan. Rescued Mao Jie from jail but unable to restore his rank. Supported Cao Pi’s succession and ascension. He Qia was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom.
